reviewNotes: Exactly three intellectual items are represented; Piers counts once and its A/B/C children are version streams.
Direct intellectual extent is 18,175 verse lines. The 18,184 raw L count includes nine NOTE1-nested quotation lines and is retained only as an encoding metric.
Richard the Redeless and The Crowned King are modeled as anonymous; Skeat's historical Langland attribution is not a modern responsibility claim, and Langland is never assigned to The Crowned King.
All 48 local copies occur exactly once across access records: 33 target-present copies with exact work portions and 15 target-absent copies without work portions.
All fourteen LMeS ZIPs are one-page partial/private-study caches of the complete highest-resolution grid, not full manuscripts and not complete multi-resolution provider pyramids.

Rights cover all 73 entities, with fourteen additional access-layer records for mediated reproduction orders; underlying text, witnesses, editions, encodings, photographs, catalog expressions, and provider terms remain distinct.
No relation says that the repository artifact consulted research sources merely because this manifest research did.

lineage.summary: The repository XML copies U-M CME item AJT8124.0001.001, which encodes Skeat's 1867 A, 1869 B, and 1873 C/appended-poems carriers. Piers Plowman is one work with A/B/C transmission streams; Richard the Redeless and The Crowned King are separate anonymous works. Skeat's A, B, and C texts use composite manuscript and print-intermediary paths, including Wright before B.15.17, Whitaker before HM 137, and three separately collated Crowley impressions. Historical witness R is one textual witness now split between Rawlinson Poetry 38 and Lansdowne 398. Forty-eight exact ignored cache objects are mapped once each, including complete codices or provider sequences where obtainable and fourteen one-page LMeS private-study tile grids.

summaries.value: A compilation of three works: Piers Plowman represented by A, B, and C version streams; the anonymous political poem Richard the Redeless; and the anonymous The Crowned King. The source XML follows Skeat's EETS carriers and includes editorial apparatus but only the direct intellectual text is counted in extent.

Piers Plowman is an expansive alliterative dream vision and allegorical estates satire concerned with Christian truth, labor, poverty, clerical conduct, penance, salvation, and just social order. The electronic item presents its A, B, and C recensions as version streams of one work.

Richard the Redeless critiques Richard II's kingship and court around the deposition crisis of 1399. The Crowned King is a short early-fifteenth-century alliterative counsel poem on kingship and governance. Both are anonymous and remain separate from Langland responsibility.

notes: The supplied title makes all three work boundaries visible and prevents the A/B/C version streams from being counted as independent works.
